sections.label: Title
sections.html:
The Flowchart Pattern.

How a whiteboard sketch becomes software that explains itself.

footprintjs · the self-explaining stack

sections.label: The Whiteboard
sections.html:
IT STARTS ON A WHITEBOARD

Every system begins as a drawing.

Start
Validate
Decide
Approve
Reject
Boxes for steps. Arrows for what happens next.

sections.label: Everyone Reads It
sections.html:
ONE PICTURE, EVERY ROLE

A PM, an engineer, and a new hire all understand it.

Start
Validate
Decide
Approve
Reject
Product
Manager
Engineer
Support
New dev
No one needs to read the code to follow the logic.

sections.label: The Drift
sections.html:
THEN WE CODE IT

But the code doesn't look like the drawing.

Product
Manager
Engineer
Support
New dev
Start
Validate
Decide
Approve
Reject
approval.service.ts 
function handle(req) {
  const ctx = load(req)
  let score = 0
  for (const r of RULES)
    if (r.match(ctx)) score += r.weight
  if (score >= T || !ctx.valid)
    return deny(reason(score))
  queue.push(ctx.id)
  return ok(sign(ctx))
}
The map and the territory drift apart.

sections.label: The Cost
sections.html:
SIX MONTHS LATER

“Why did it do that?”

?
14:22:07.118 INFO load ctx id=8841
14:22:07.142 WARN rule miss r=7
14:22:07.143 INFO risk=7 limit=10
14:22:07.144 approve 8841 · why??
audit.log(8841, approve)
… 2,193 more lines
Product
Manager
Engineer
Support
New dev
The drawing is gone; the answer is buried in logs.

sections.label: Scales To Agents
sections.html:
BACKEND → AI AGENTS

The same pattern wraps an AI agent.

request
LLMreason()

answer

toollookupPolicy → allow
llm“allowed → approve”

The hardest “why did it do that?” — answered by construction.
